title: The United States after 2000
description: Check your understanding of the history of the United States after the year 2000.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Al-Qaeda**, a terrorist group whose name means “The Base” or “The Foundation” in Arabic, is most notorious for

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Al-Qaeda was not involved in Iraq's 1990 invasion of Kuwait.

choices.content: organizing the September 11th attacks in New York, the Pentagon, and Flight 93 over Pennsylvania.
invading Kuwait in 1990.
taking control of Iraq and Syria in the wake of civil war in 2013.

assessment_items.question_content:
What was unexpected about President George W. Bush’s support for programs like **No Child Left Behind** and **Medicare Part D**?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: First Lady Laura Bush did not oppose No Child Left Behind or Medicare Part D.

choices.content: Those two pieces of legislation were much more aligned with traditional liberal policies that expanded federal spending, running counter to his conservative philosophy.
Both programs left key policy decisions up to the states, and as a politician Bush had generally favored a stronger central government.
Bush's wife, Laura, strongly opposed both programs, leading to an unusual policy rift between the First Lady and the president.

assessment_items.question_content:
Why was the **Department of Homeland Security** formed?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Department of Homeland Security was not created to prevent police violence in cities.

choices.content: To coordinate American efforts to prevent future terrorist attacks like the one on September 11th, 2001.
To prevent police violence in American cities.
To replace the TSA (Transportation Security Administration), which was seen as ineffective.

assessment_items.question_content:
What powers did the **USA Patriot Act** grant to the federal government?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Japanese internment was the result of Executive Order 9066, issued by Franklin D. Roosevelt in 1942, not the USA Patriot Act.

choices.content: Expanded surveillance powers to wiretap American citizens suspected of terrorism, anti-money-laundering powers to fight the financing of terrorism, among others.
The power to remove citizens of Japanese ancestry from the West Coast and intern them in remote camps when judged a military necessity.
The power to declare that parcels of land near national borders were "areas of military necessity" and confiscate them without compensating their owners.

assessment_items.question_content:
What proved so controversial about the Bush administration’s allegations about Iraq’s stockpile of **weapons of mass destruction** or **WMDs**?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: Iraq's stockpile of WMDs was not larger than expected.

choices.content: Following the invasion of Iraq, inspectors from the United Nations never found evidence of WMDs in Iraq.
Following an inquiry, UN inspectors discovered that the United States government itself had supplied the WMDs to Iraq.
UN inspectors discovered that Iraq's stockpile of WMDs was considerably larger than the Bush administration had projected, leading to accusations of military mismanagement.

assessment_items.question_content:
How did the Bush administration justify its 2003 **invasion of Iraq**?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Bush administration did not claim that the government of Iraq itself had masterminded the September 11, 2001 terror attacks.

choices.content: It claimed that Iraqi dictator Saddam Hussein had stockpiled weapons of mass destruction, and that his government had supported Al-Qaeda.
It claimed that the government of Iraq had masterminded the 2001 terror attacks on the United States.
It claimed that Iraq had supported the regime of Bashar al-Assad in Syria.

assessment_items.question_content:
How did President Obama’s administration attempt to mitigate the effects of the **Great Recession**?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Hawley-Smoot tariff was passed in 1930, and many historians consider it one of the reasons why the Great Depression lasted for so long.

choices.content: President Obama proposed and signed an economic stimulus bill into law; his administration authorized federal spending on education, green energy, and infrastructure improvements.
President Obama proposed broad tax cuts in an effort to stimulate businesses through "supply-side" economics.
President Obama imposed new trade restrictions and tariffs that protected American industry; the Hawley-Smoot tariff raised rates on more than 20,000 imported goods.

assessment_items.question_content:
Which of the following factors contributed to the **Great Recession** of 2008? 

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The stock market crash on October 29 happened in 1929 and caused the Great Depression, not the Great Recession!

choices.content: The collapse of a "housing bubble" and the failure of Wall Street investment firm Lehmen Brothers.
The collapse of the 1990s "dotcom" bubble and and the rise of globalization.
The stock market crash on October 29th and the subsequent failure of many banks.

assessment_items.question_content:
Which of the following were among the provisions of the **Affordable Care Act**?

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints: The Affordable Care Act did not involve the federal government taking over the health care system entirely.

choices.content: The law made health insurance coverage mandatory for American citizens, and made it illegal for insurance companies to deny coverage for preexisting conditions.
The law made it illegal for health insurance companies to charge "unaffordable" rates for individual insurance, which was defined as more than 10% of an individual's take-home pay.
The law created a nationalized health system in which the government took over management of all hospitals and health insurance.
